title: Foc la răsărit: Iranul și noul război din Orientul Mijlociu
summary: On February 28, 2026, the US and Israel launched simultaneous large-scale military operations against Iran's nuclear sites, military infrastructure, and leadership, killing Ayatollah Khamenei. Iran retaliated with ballistic missiles targeting Israel and US bases, sending shockwaves across Gulf states and raising fears of a wider regional conflict and energy crisis.
talking_points: The US ('Operation Epic Fury') and Israel ('Roaring Lion') conducted coordinated preemptive strikes on Iran's nuclear facilities, military command centers, and leadership, eliminating Ayatollah Khamenei
Israel's justification draws on defensive realism and the deep-rooted preemptive doctrine rooted in existential threat perception, while the US cited the need to neutralize WMD capabilities
Iran's retaliatory strikes hit Gulf states, creating an unexpected coalition of Arab nations united against Tehran
The Strait of Hormuz chokepoint poses a critical risk to global energy markets, with insurance costs surging and maritime traffic disruption fears growing
The legality of the preemptive strike under international law is questionable under UN Charter Article 51, though supporters argue unilateral action is justified when multilateral institutions fail
author: Diana Florentina Năsulea
